name: Paystack
what_it_does: Paystack is a developer-friendly African payment gateway (acquired by Stripe) that enables businesses to accept card, bank transfer, mobile money, and USSD payments across Nigeria, Ghana, South Africa, and Kenya.
use_cases: Initialize and verify card charges programmatically to build custom checkout flows
Create recurring subscription plans and manage customer payment authorizations
Automate bulk payouts to multiple vendors or contractors via the Transfers API
Reconcile settled transactions by polling the Transactions and Settlements APIs
Generate and dispatch payment pages or links for no-code invoice collection
not_for: Accepting payments outside the four supported African markets — not a global payment solution
Businesses that cannot meet Nigerian or Ghanaian merchant verification requirements
best_when: Building payment infrastructure for African e-commerce, SaaS, or marketplace platforms that need clean REST APIs, strong documentation, and Stripe-grade developer experience within supported markets.
avoid_when: Your customer base spans multiple continents or you need settlement in currencies beyond NGN, GHS, ZAR, or KES.

auth.notes: Secret key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header; public key used for client-side SDK initialization. Separate keys for test and live environments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No monthly fee; pay-per-transaction from the first payment
pricing.paid_tiers: 1.5% + NGN 100 per local transaction (capped at NGN 2,000); international cards at 3.9% + NGN 100
pricing.notes: No setup or monthly fees. Transactions below NGN 2,500 are not subject to the NGN 100 flat fee in Nigeria.

performance.rate_limits: No hard published rate limit; practical throughput is several hundred requests per minute

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include a boolean status, human-readable message, and data field; among the clearest error formats of any African gateway.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transaction reference field provides idempotency for charge initialization; no explicit idempotency-key header for all endpoints.
known_agent_gotchas: Charge initialization returns a hosted URL that requires user interaction — not fully headless for new cards without a stored authorization
Stored card authorization codes expire if a customer's card is renewed; agents must handle auth reuse failures gracefully
Webhook IP whitelisting is recommended but not required; always verify HMAC signature before processing events
Live and test environments are completely separate — switching requires updating the secret key, not just a flag
Bulk transfer jobs are asynchronous; agents must poll the Transfer or listen for transfer.success/transfer.failed webhooks rather than assuming immediate settlement

security_score_components.security_notes: Single secret key with no scope granularity; webhook events should be verified using the X-Paystack-Signature HMAC-SHA512 header to prevent event spoofing.
